evals.name: standalone-creation
evals.expected_output: Detects no handoff artifact (wip/vision_new-developer-portal_scope.md absent). Starts from Phase 1 (scope). Asks scoping questions about the project thesis, audience, and org fit. Produces a VISION doc at docs/visions/VISION-new-developer-portal.md with all 7 required sections in correct order. Thesis section is phrased as a hypothesis, not a problem statement.
expectations: Plan checks for wip/vision_new-developer-portal_scope.md handoff artifact before deciding which phase to start from
Plan starts from Phase 0 (setup/branch creation) or Phase 1 (scope) since no handoff artifact exists
Plan includes a conversational scoping step (Phase 1) before any research or drafting
Plan includes Phase 2 (discover) with parallel research agents
Plan includes Phase 3 (draft) producing a VISION document at docs/visions/VISION-new-developer-portal.md
Plan includes Phase 4 (validate) with jury review
Plan mentions reading phase reference files (e.g. references/phases/phase-1-scope.md)
Plan mentions detecting repo visibility from CLAUDE.md or repo path

evals.name: explore-handoff-detection
evals.expected_output: Detects existing handoff artifact at wip/vision_workflow-engine_scope.md. Skips Phase 1 (scope) and resumes at Phase 2 (discover). Uses the handoff artifact's problem statement, research leads, and decisions as input. Does not re-ask scoping questions that were already answered during /explore.
expectations: Plan checks for wip/vision_workflow-engine_scope.md handoff artifact
Plan detects the existing handoff artifact and skips Phase 1 (scope)
Plan resumes at Phase 2 (discover) using the handoff artifact content
Plan does NOT include conversational scoping questions since handoff provides them
Plan mentions reading the handoff artifact for problem statement and research leads

evals.name: visibility-gating-public
evals.expected_output: Detects Public visibility from CLAUDE.md. Produces a VISION doc with 7 required sections. Does NOT include Competitive Positioning or Resource Implications sections (private-only). All content is appropriate for public consumption.
expectations: Plan detects Public visibility from CLAUDE.md or repo path
Plan explicitly excludes Competitive Positioning section from the VISION doc
Plan explicitly excludes Resource Implications section from the VISION doc
Plan mentions reading skills/public-content/SKILL.md for content governance
Plan includes all 7 required sections (Status, Thesis, Audience, Value Proposition, Org Fit, Success Criteria, Non-Goals)
Plan acknowledges --auto flag for non-interactive execution mode

evals.name: lifecycle-accept-verb
evals.expected_output: Detects lifecycle verb 'accept'. Validates Open Questions section is empty or removed. Runs transition-status.sh to transition from Draft to Accepted. Reports success. File stays in docs/visions/ (no directory movement for Accepted).
expectations: Plan detects 'accept' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow
Plan includes validating that Open Questions section is empty or removed before transition
Plan mentions running scripts/transition-status.sh to perform the transition
Plan targets transitioning from Draft to Accepted status
Plan does NOT move the file to a different directory (file stays in docs/visions/)

evals.name: lifecycle-sunset-with-move
evals.expected_output: Detects lifecycle verb 'sunset'. Runs transition-status.sh to transition from Active to Sunset. File is moved to docs/visions/sunset/VISION-old-project.md via git mv. Status updated in both frontmatter and body.
expectations: Plan detects 'sunset' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow
Plan mentions running scripts/transition-status.sh to perform the transition
Plan includes moving the file to docs/visions/sunset/ directory
Plan mentions the transition is from Active to Sunset status
Plan mentions git mv or file movement for the directory change

evals.name: jury-review-quality
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 spawns 3 jury agents focused on vision-specific quality. Agents check: thesis is a hypothesis (not problem statement), success criteria are project-level (not feature-level), org fit explains why HERE, non-goals are about identity (not scope), content boundaries are respected (no features, sequencing, or architecture).
expectations: Plan includes Phase 4 (validate) with jury review agents
Plan mentions jury checking that thesis is a hypothesis not a problem statement
Plan mentions jury checking that success criteria are project-level not feature-level
Plan mentions jury checking org fit explains why HERE
Plan mentions jury checking non-goals are about identity not just scope
Plan mentions jury checking content boundaries (no features, sequencing, or architecture)

evals.name: empty-argument-prompts
evals.expected_output: With no argument, skill asks the user what project they want to create a vision for. Does not assume a topic or start drafting immediately.
expectations: Plan includes asking the user what project or org they want to define a vision for
Plan does NOT assume a topic or start drafting immediately
Plan does NOT proceed to Phase 1 scoping without first getting a topic from the user

evals.name: crystallize-discrimination
evals.prompt: /explore --auto --strategic should we build a developer analytics dashboard
evals.expected_output: Exploration in strategic scope. Crystallize framework scores VISION higher than PRD because: project doesn't exist yet, exploration centered on 'should we build this', org fit is the core question. VISION signals fire, PRD anti-signals fire (no existing project). Routes to /vision via Phase 5 handoff.
expectations: Plan involves an exploration workflow, not a direct /vision invocation
Plan includes a crystallize or artifact-type selection step
Plan scores or routes toward VISION as the appropriate artifact type
Plan reasoning mentions that the project does not exist yet as a signal for VISION over PRD
Plan mentions Phase 5 handoff to /vision after exploration completes
